observation: Task 832 ran a chore to verify the existing drizzle-orm SQL injection vulnerability already tracked in issue #832. All 7 pipeline stages completed with 0 retries, and PR #833 was successfully shipped. However, the verify stage reported FAIL with TypeScript typecheck errors in .next/dev/types/validator.ts (PagesPageConfig constraint violations), yet the pipeline proceeded to ship anyway. This suggests the verify stage failures are informational rather than blocking.
patternMatch: This matches a known pattern from previous runs where pipeline verification failures do not block deployment. The system shipped PR #833 despite verify-stage typecheck failures, consistent with observation #4224 documenting that pipeline verification stage does not block deployment despite failures.
suggestion: Configure the pipeline to block shipping when verify stage fails with typecheck errors, or add a conditional check that prevents autofix/ship stages from running when verify reports FAIL status.

pipelineFlaw.issue: Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage
pipelineFlaw.evidence: verify.md shows result:FAIL with typecheck errors in validator.ts, but ship.md shows PR #833 was created successfully, indicating the pipeline continued despite verification failure

observation: Task 841 completed a chore to verify existing FTS search functionality, with all pipeline stages (taskify, plan, build, verify, review, review-fix, ship) completing in 500s with zero retries across all stages. The verify stage reported FAIL status due to TypeScript typecheck errors in .next/dev/types/validator.ts involving PagesPageConfig type constraints for modal.ts and ErrorPage.test imports. Despite verify failures, the pipeline proceeded to create PR #843, indicating the ship stage was not gated on verify status.
patternMatch: This matches the identical pattern from task 832 (retro 2026-04-08) where verify-stage typecheck failures did not block deployment. Consistent with observation #4224 documenting that the pipeline verification stage does not block deployment despite failures.
suggestion: Add a conditional gate in the ship stage that checks verify stage exit status and halts with a clear error message when verify reports FAIL, rather than proceeding silently to ship.

pipelineFlaw.issue: Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage
pipelineFlaw.evidence: verify.md shows FAIL result with typecheck errors (PagesPageConfig type mismatch in validator.ts lines 350, 386, 395), yet ship stage completed successfully and PR #843 was created. This is identical to the failure pattern from task 832.

observation: Task 260409-111824 successfully created an isOdd utility function in src/utils/math.ts with all 7 pipeline stages completing in 212 seconds and zero retries across all stages. However, the verify stage reported FAIL with typecheck errors in .next/dev/types/validator.ts (TS2344 type constraint failures on modal and ErrorPage.test imports), yet the ship stage proceeded to completion without blocking. This run exhibits the same verify-then-ship pattern failure documented in retrospectives for runs 832 and 841, where verification failures do not prevent pipeline progression to the ship stage.
patternMatch: This is the third consecutive instance of the identical pattern (runs 832, 841, and now 260409-111824) where verify-stage typecheck failures fail to block deployment. The previous two retrospectives both recommended adding a conditional gate in ship to check verify exit status, but the flaw persists unchanged, indicating the recommendation was not implemented.
suggestion: Implement the conditional gate in the ship stage that was recommended in the two prior retrospectives: check verify stage exit status and halt with a clear error message when verify reports FAIL, rather than proceeding silently to ship.

pipelineFlaw.issue: Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage
pipelineFlaw.evidence: verify.md shows Result: FAIL with multiple TS2344 typecheck errors in .next/dev/types/validator.ts, yet ship stage completed with 0 retries indicating normal progression. This is the third consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824) exhibiting the same behavior pattern.

observation: Task 840-260409-110822 completed a chore to verify the memory nudge feature end-to-end, with all 7 pipeline stages completing in 1018s. The verify stage reported FAIL due to typecheck errors in .next/dev/types/validator.ts involving PagesPageConfig constraints for modal and ErrorPage imports. The review and review-fix stages completed successfully (0 retries each), but the verify stage still failed. The pipeline proceeded to ship, updating PR #843, demonstrating the fourth consecutive instance of the identical pattern where verify-stage typecheck failures do not prevent deployment.
patternMatch: This is the fourth consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824, and now 840) exhibiting the same pattern: verify stage reports FAIL due to typecheck errors, yet the pipeline continues to review-fix and ship without blocking. The autofix stage completes successfully but cannot fix PagesPageConfig type constraint violations that appear to be structural page configuration mismatches. Despite repeated retrospectives recommending a conditional gate in ship to check verify exit status, the flaw persists unchanged.
suggestion: Investigate whether autofix has the capability to resolve PagesPageConfig type constraint violations in .next/dev/types/validator.ts, or if these require manual intervention. If autofix cannot address this class of errors, configure the pipeline to block ship stage when verify reports FAIL for typecheck errors specifically.

pipelineFlaw.issue: Verify stage typecheck failures do not halt pipeline progression to ship, and autofix cannot resolve PagesPageConfig type constraint violations
pipelineFlaw.evidence: verify.md shows FAIL with type errors at validator.ts lines 350 and 386 involving PagesPageConfig constraints; all stages including review-fix completed with 0 retries; ship.md confirms PR #843 was updated despite verify FAIL; fourth consecutive run demonstrating this pattern

observation: Task 842-260409-112718 completed a chore to verify existing MCP server integration implementation, with all 7 pipeline stages completing successfully including verify which required 1 retry. However, the verify.md artifact shows verification FAIL due to persistent PagesPageConfig type constraint errors in .next/dev/types/validator.ts (lines 350, 386, 395) that appear to be structural page configuration mismatches. The pipeline nonetheless proceeded to ship stage and updated PR #843, continuing the established pattern where typecheck failures do not block deployment.
patternMatch: This is the fifth consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, and now 842) exhibiting the identical pattern: verify stage reports FAIL due to PagesPageConfig type constraint violations in .next/dev/types/validator.ts, yet the pipeline proceeds through review-fix and ship without blocking. Four prior retrospectives recommended adding a conditional gate in ship to check verify exit status, but the flaw persists unchanged. The autofix stage cannot resolve this class of structural type errors, and the pipeline lacks a mechanism to halt when verify reports FAIL.
suggestion: Since four consecutive retrospectives have recommended implementing a conditional gate in ship to check verify exit status without implementation, escalate this to a configuration change rather than another recommendation: add a verifyExitCode check in the ship stage template that reads the verify stage's exit status and halts with an error when verify reports FAIL, preventing automatic shipment of code that failed typecheck validation.

pipelineFlaw.issue: Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage
pipelineFlaw.evidence: verify.md shows verification FAIL with typecheck errors for PagesPageConfig type constraints, yet ship.md shows PR #843 was successfully shipped. This is the fifth consecutive run (runs 832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, 842) exhibiting this exact behavior pattern, with four prior retrospectives recommending the same fix (conditional gate in ship) but no changes made.
